title: Feedback Speed

content.question: Where are you getting feedback too slowly to act on it?
sop: List your key Feedback loops, where you measure results and where you don't (see calculation, page 5)
Rate the Speed 1-10 of each loop, how quickly you actually see the result of an action (see calculation, page 5)
Shorten the slowest loop, check it weekly or daily instead of monthly.
Rate your Adjustment 1-10, how much you actually change behavior based on what you see (see calculation, page 5)
If Adjustment is low despite fast Speed, fix that first, fast feedback you ignore is wasted.
questions: Where are you getting feedback too slowly to act on it?
What decision are you still making on data that's a month old?
If you got results on today's actions by tomorrow morning instead of next quarter, what would you do differently right now?

definitions.def: When a system's output is fed back as input, allowing self-correction or self-amplification.

content.equation_example: Rate Speed 1-10 (how fast you get feedback) and Adjustment 1-10 (how well you act on it). Speed 8 times Adjustment 5 equals 40 out of 100. Higher means fast feedback is actually translating into real course-correction. Lower means either feedback is slow, or you're not adjusting even when it's fast.
content.closing_statement: Fast feedback you ignore is the same as no feedback.

caption:
Principle Nº 145: Feedback Speed

Faster feedback = faster improvement.

Ask yourself: Where are you getting feedback too slowly to act on it?
